title: Protocol: Quasi-experimental analysis of PEPFAR effects on non-HIV clinical trial activity in Africa
body: This quasi-experimental study will estimate the causal effect of PEPFAR on non-HIV trial starts in recipient African countries using three methods. We will query ClinicalTrials.gov for annual interventional trial counts from 2000 to 2025 across ten PEPFAR-focus countries and five non-PEPFAR comparators. The primary analysis is an interrupted time series with segmented regression estimating level change and slope change at the 2004 PEPFAR implementation point. Secondary analyses include difference-in-differences comparing annual trial growth between treatment and control groups across pre-defined windows, and synthetic control constructing a counterfactual Uganda from weighted non-PEPFAR donors. Sensitivity analyses will vary the post-period window. All query logic, country classifications, and models will be prespecified and version-controlled. No patient-level data will be used. Limitations include ecological design, registration bias around the 2005 ICMJE mandate, a short pre-period, and inability to separate PEPFAR effects from concurrent Global Fund and bilateral aid programs.

sentences.text: This study will estimate the causal effect of PEPFAR on non-HIV clinical trial starts in recipient African countries using three quasi-experimental methods.
We will query ClinicalTrials.gov for annual interventional trial counts from 2000 to 2025 across ten PEPFAR-focus and five non-PEPFAR African comparator countries.
The primary analysis uses interrupted time series with segmented regression at the 2004 PEPFAR implementation point.
Secondary analyses include difference-in-differences across pre-defined time windows and a synthetic control method for Uganda using non-PEPFAR donor weights.
Sensitivity analyses will vary the post-period window definition and trial category classification.
All query logic, country classifications, and statistical models will be prespecified and version-controlled with no patient-level data used.
Limitations include ecological design, registration bias around the 2005 ICMJE mandate, short pre-period, and inability to separate PEPFAR from concurrent aid programs.

outsideNote.data: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2, 10 PEPFAR-focus + 5 non-PEPFAR countries, 2000-2025

ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for code generation and protocol drafting. The author specified all methodological choices, reviewed the protocol, and takes full responsibility for the content.

references.desc: Bendavid & Bhatt (2009). Does PEPFAR crowd out other health aid?
Abadie, Diamond & Hainmueller (2010). Synthetic Control Methods.
Bernal, Cummins & Gasparrini (2017). ITS design for policy evaluation.
